description:
You are acting as an observability engineer doing a quick-and-dirty micro-benchmark to verify that a new dashboard’s latency panels display the right units (seconds, to three decimal places).  
    Please create a small benchmark report that measures three well-known, deterministic shell commands and records their wall-clock run-times in a CSV file.

    Scope of the benchmark
    ----------------------
    1. sleep 0.5          – label it in the report as sleep_0.5  
    2. sleep 1            – label it in the report as sleep_1  
    3. sleep 0.2          – label it in the report as sleep_0.2  

    Output requirements
    -------------------
    1. Create a directory called /home/user/perf.  
    2. Inside that directory create the file /home/user/perf/benchmark.csv.  
    3. The CSV **must** contain exactly four lines: one header line followed by one line per command, in this order:

       command,elapsed_seconds  
       sleep_0.5,0.500  
       sleep_1,1.000  
       sleep_0.2,0.200  

       • “command” is the literal label shown above  
       • “elapsed_seconds” is the wall-clock runtime printed with exactly three digits after the decimal point (zero-padded if necessary).  
       • Use **LF** line endings, no trailing blanks, no extra spaces, and ensure the file ends with a single newline.  

    4. Do not store the raw timing data anywhere else; only benchmark.csv should remain when you finish.

    Verification hint
    -----------------
    Automated tests will look for:
    • The existence of /home/user/perf/benchmark.csv  
    • The file’s exact contents and formatting (header + 3 data rows, values as shown, precise 3-decimal formatting).  
    • Correct pathname and case sensitivity.

    Remember: you do not have root access, and you should rely solely on standard POSIX tools that are pre-installed in a minimal Linux environment (e.g., /usr/bin/time, printf, echo, etc.).
truth:
Directory that must exist once the agent completes:
    • /home/user/perf      (default 755 permissions)

    File that must exist once the agent completes:
    • /home/user/perf/benchmark.csv      (default 644 permissions)

    Exact, byte-for-byte contents of /home/user/perf/benchmark.csv:

command,elapsed_seconds
sleep_0.5,0.500
sleep_1,1.000
sleep_0.2,0.200
name: task_000000_3c673e1f
